newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Stock drifted -0.47% on June 9 (flow, not fundamental). Early-June UBS Buy rating adds sell-side visibility but no new evidence; the AI-robotics/semiconductor demand narrative dates to the April FY guidance (next earnings July 3, 2026).

sources.label: Digitimes - Yaskawa sees AI robot, chip demand boost profits (April 2026)
Yaskawa global news page (no fresh June releases)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down move already priced the rerating penalty (adjusted-score rank 77 vs displayed 65); nothing new since June 6 and today's -0.47% drift does not change the setup. On a latency-alpha lens, a +28.8% YTD large-cap with a UBS Buy is well-discovered, not latent, so a small negative drift is warranted but no further rank move.
verdicts.bull: Servo motors and motion control are a genuine bottleneck for AI-enabled factories and humanoid robotics, and FY guidance calls for order growth driven by AI-related demand with ~10.3% operating margin.
verdicts.neutral: Known automation cyclical with the AI-robotics story already in the price after +29% YTD; next real catalyst is the July 3 earnings print, so the name marks time until then.
verdicts.bear: China capex remains the swing factor; if the AI-robotics order inflection disappoints at July earnings, the YTD rerating unwinds and the cyclical discount reasserts itself.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q1 FY2026 earnings on July 3, 2026 confirming AI-driven order acceleration in robotics and motion control.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded, well-covered large-cap cyclical whose AI narrative is consensus; China/auto capex softness or a guidance miss unwinds the rerating.
